I'm sitting on a pair of Ore from my mountain home, but a true empire isn't built on stone alone—it needs the flexibility of Timber and Fiber. I'm going to cast a wide net with the neighbors, P1 and P2, offering my Ore to see who bites for a Timber or Fiber trade. If they accept, I diversify my holdings; if not, I still have my Ore. Regardless of the chatter, I must act. I'll head to the Plains to secure Timber, a vital component for the Sh

Round 1, volume game. I have 2 Fiber (F) at home. I need to diversify to hit building costs (Shack needs S, Den needs non-home). I'll propose a swap of 1F for 1T or 1O to anyone—cheap entry to get resources moving. Then I'll hit Plains to grab Timber (T) and start the engine. No time to scout; time is money.

The table is quiet, and silence is the enemy of empire. I'm sitting on a modest stack of Timber and Ore, but I need Fiber to unlock a Den or Watchtower. I could grind it out myself, but why not spark a conversation? I'll propose a trade to P2, offering them my spare Timber in exchange for Fiber. If they bite, we both get closer to building, and I secure a partner. If not, I'll head to the swamps to gather Fiber myself—still progress, just less social. Let's see who wants to deal.
